LAW AND MOTION 301, THIRD PARTIES T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, LEGAL RECOVER LLC, J1K5 LLC, AND VI TRAN, MOVE THIS COURT TO SET ASIDE ITS ORDER OF MAY 28, 2025 ADDING T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, AND LEGAL RECOVER LLC AS JUDGMENT DEBTORS IN THIS ACTION. THIS ORDER WAS APPEALED ON JULY 28, 2025. THIS COURT THEREFORE LACKS JURISDICTION OVER THE ORDER; "THE PERFECTING OF AN APPEAL STAYS PROCEEDINGS IN THE TRIAL COURT UPON THE JUDGMENT OR ORDER APPEALED FROM OR UPON THE MATTERS EMBRACED THEREIN OR AFFECTED THEREBY." (CODE CIV. PROC., SEC. 916, SUBD. (A).) MOVING PARTIES RELY ON THE RULE THAT IF A JUDGMENT "IS VOID UPON ITS FACE, IT MAY BE SET ASIDE AT ANY TIME," REGARDLESS OF WHETHER AN APPEAL IS PENDING. (FALLON V. SUPERIOR COURT OF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (1939) 33 CAL.APP.2D 48, 51.) BUT THE ORDER THEY SEEK TO SET ASIDE IS NOT VOID ON ITS FACE; MOVING PARTIES SEEK TO SET ASIDE THE ORDER BASED ON EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE, TO WIT, INFORMATION PROVIDED IN THE LEEDS DECLARATION. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. . JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Feb-25-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

THIRD PARTY T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, LEGAL RECOVER LLC, J1K5 LLC, VI TRAN'S Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Amend Judgment To Add T24 LLC, Legal Recovery LLC, And Legal Recover LLC As Judgment Debtors [CCP Section 473 Subd. (D)].

Third parties T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, LEGAL RECOVER LLC, J1K5 LLC, and VI TRAN, move this court to set aside its order of May 28, 2025 adding T24 LLC, Legal Recovery LLC, and Legal Recover LLC as judgment debtors in this action. This order was appealed on July 28, 2025. This court therefore lacks jurisdiction over the order; "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court upon the judgment or order appealed from or upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 916, subd. (a).) Moving parties rely on the rule that if a judgment "is void upon its face, it may be set aside at any time," regardless of whether an appeal is pending. (Fallon v. Superior Court of City and County of San Francisco (1939) 33 Cal.App.2d 48, 51.) But the order they seek to set aside is not void on its face; moving parties seek to set aside the order based on extrinsic evidence, to wit, information provided in the Leeds declaration.

proposed order submitted.

DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion To Tax Costs.

Plaintiffs Cyrstal Lei And Bryant Fu'S Motion For Order Setting Attorneys Fees In Favor Of Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Crystal Lei & Bryant Fu 415-221-0969 Plaintiffs in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CRYSTAL LEI, BRYANT FU's Motion For Order Setting Attorneys Fees In Favor Of Plaintiffs.

Plaintiffs Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu's motion for an order setting attorneys fees in favor of Plaintiffs and against Defendant Demas Yan is granted in the amount of $42,687.69. Plaintiffs prevailed on Yan's anti-SLAPP special motion to strike, and this Court awarded attorneys' fees on the ground that the special motion was frivolous. (See Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(c).) The Court of Appeal affirmed that order in its entirety, finding that Yan's anti-SLAPP motion was a bad faith delay tactic. "A statute authorizing an attorney fee award at the trial court level includes appellate attorney fees unless the statute specifically provides otherwise." (Evans v. Unkow (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1490, 1499.) "Section 425.16, subdivision (c), does not preclude recovery of appellate attorney fees; hence attorney fees recoverable under the statute include appellate fees." (Carpenter v. Jack in the Box Corp. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 454, 461.) Thus, "[t]he trial court's authority to award fees and costs under section 425.16, subdivision (c), includes authority to award fees incurred in responding to an appeal of an order granting or denying a special motion to strike, or of an order awarding attorney fees in connection with such motion." (Id.; see also, e.g., Baharian-Mehr v. Smith (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 265, 275 [plaintiff's attorney fees on appeal from order finding anti-SLAPP motion frivolous are "recoverable under the statute"]; Taxpayer and Consumer Rights v. Garamendi (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 1375, 1395 [same].) Yan shall pay the fees awarded within 10 days of entry of the Court's order.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
